summary: Large gm_1A4C decompilation pass that replaced many placeholder function stubs and UNK prototypes with typed implementations, added local/static data definitions, corrected related headers and symbols, and produced 47 new matches plus 11 partial improvements in GALE01. The work centers on game-mode/title/movie/scene flow code, including MTHP playback frame logic, title/opening scene setup, HSD object animation callbacks, SIS/SObj rendering helpers, player/stage setup, and cleanup/transition handlers. Author noted that several not-quite-matching functions should become 100% once split into their appropriate files.
what_changed: Implemented many previously stubbed gm_1A4C functions, including gm_801A6308, gm_801A64A8_OnFrame, gm_801A659C, gm_801A6630, gm_801A68D8, animation callbacks such as fn_801A6844/fn_801A7A44/fn_801A80CC/fn_801A80F0, title/opening handlers such as gm_801AA110_OnEnter and gm_801AA28C_OnFrame, and later scene/on-frame/on-enter helpers such as gm_801ACCA0_OnEnter, gm_801ACD8C_OnFrame, gm_801ACE94_OnEnter, gm_801ACF8C_OnFrame, gm_801AD088, gm_801AD254, gm_801AD620_OnFrame, gm_801ADB04, gm_801AE640, gm_801AE74C, gm_801AE848, and fn_801AE948.

Added typed static globals and data in gm_1A4C.c/static.h, including MTHP/opening state bytes and counters, HSD_GObj pointers, HSD_ImageDesc buffers, Vec3 camera/player coordinate constants gm_803DB2C8 and gm_803DB2D4, float scale table gm_803DB2EC, opening movie parameter table gm_803DBFB4, and more precise nested structs for gm_80480D58, gm_804D67F8, gm_804D67FC, gm_804D680C, and unkd4d0.

Reworked gm_1A4C includes from older relative forms like baselib/sislib.h and gm/gmmain_lib.h to explicit <melee/...> and <sysdolphin/baselib/...> include paths, adding needed dependencies for camera, db, ef, ftDemo, item, ground/stage, player, mpcoll, CObj/DObj/SObj/particle/display/gobjplink, and related libraries.

Updated gm_1A4C.h from broad UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations to concrete signatures for many functions, including bool returns, void callbacks with HSD_GObj*, int/s16 arguments, gm_801A9DD0(HSD_GObj*, u16, int, int, int), and gm_801AE050 returning bool.

Adjusted config/GALE01/symbols.txt by marking gm_803DB2C8 and gm_803DB2D4 as float data and splitting a former 0x50 string blob at gm_803DD408 into several lbl_803DD408/lbl_803DD414/lbl_803DD430/lbl_803DD43C/lbl_803DD44C string symbols, while correcting nearby string sizes.
Changed gm_1B03 storage/prototypes around gm_8049C178: static s8 array became u8, and gm_801BEFB0, gm_801BF010, and gm_801BF050 now return int in both source and header.

Corrected related API prototypes needed by gm_1A4C: lbMthp_8001F410 now takes a filename and arguments, lbMthp_8001F624 takes HSD_GObj* and dimensions, lbMthp_8001F67C takes HSD_GObj* and int, mn_8022F0F0 takes int, hsd_80392528 takes Event, and gmTitle_801A12C4 returns UNK_T.

Changed HSD_SisLib_803A611C to take HSD_GObj* instead of u32, updated call sites in grstadium.c and un_2FC9.c to pass GObj pointers without casts, and used NULL comparison instead of integer zero for the GObj argument.
Added SObj library declarations for HSD_SObjLib_803A477C, HSD_SObjLib_803A49E0, and HSD_SObjLib_803A54EC to support the new gm_1A4C SObj rendering/copy-from-EFB code.
smart_moves: The PR did not only add C bodies; it fixed surrounding types and prototypes so the new code could be expressed naturally with HSD_GObj*, HSD_CObj*, HSD_JObj*, HSD_LObj*, HSD_ImageDesc, Event, Vec3, and bool instead of opaque UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS.
Correcting HSD_SisLib_803A611C from u32 to HSD_GObj* removed pointer-as-integer casts at multiple call sites and made the NULL branch in sislib.c semantically clear.

Splitting data symbols and annotating float data in symbols.txt made the emitted data layout match the source definitions for Vec3/float tables instead of treating adjacent strings/data as one coarse blob.
The author used small targeted matching aids such as PAD_STACK entries and #pragma dont_inline around gm_801A659C instead of over-broad code reshaping.
Static.h was used to document inferred static state and nested layout where full type names were still unknown, preserving offsets while giving fields enough type information for matching work.
The bot report shows this was an efficient match-yielding pass: +8320 matched bytes, 47 new 100% matches, and 11 improved unmatched functions.
decomp_lessons: When a game-mode file calls broad engine/library APIs, improving the callee prototypes can be necessary for matching the caller; gm_1A4C needed lbMthp, SIS, SObj, mn, particle, gmTitle, and gm_1B03 signature fixes.
If a function uses a GObj pointer as an API handle, type it as HSD_GObj* rather than u32 and update callers to remove casts; this improves both C readability and matching stability.
For data-heavy game-mode files, symbol slicing matters: split separate strings and tables in symbols.txt instead of leaving a single large string/data symbol when source wants distinct objects.
Frame-driven movie/title code often matches through explicit static counters/flags and exact threshold constants; preserve timer variables as u8/u32/s32 according to observed use.

HSD animation callback patterns are reusable: JObj callbacks usually call HSD_JObjAnimAll(GET_JOBJ(gobj)), LObj callbacks call HSD_LObjAnimAll(GET_LOBJ(gobj)), and CObj callbacks animate then compare aobj curr_frame/end_frame for transition cleanup.

Partial matches may be blocked by file ownership/data placement. The author explicitly noted that several functions that were not quite matching should reach 100% once split into their appropriate files.
Use local anonymous or placeholder-named structs in static headers when field offsets are known but semantic names are not; this can unblock matches without inventing false domain names.
naming_conventions: Address-derived game-mode symbols retain gm_801Axxxx / fn_801Axxxx names when semantic names are not yet known.
Scene lifecycle callbacks use suffixes such as _OnEnter, _OnFrame, and _OnLeave.
Static/global data keeps address-derived gm_803Dxxxx or gm_804Dxxxx names unless split out as generic lbl_803Dxxxx symbols for local strings.
Header prototypes preserve address comments such as /* 1A659C */ next to each declaration.
HSD object variables are typed and named around HSD_GObj, HSD_CObj, HSD_JObj, HSD_LObj, HSD_ImageDesc, and HSD_Text rather than left as raw integers or void pointers where evidence exists.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop was applied around gm_801A659C to preserve the expected call/code shape.

PAD_STACK macros were inserted in several functions with known stack-shape requirements, including gm_801A68D8, fn_801A6ACC, fn_801A6D78, fn_801A94BC, gm_801AA110_OnEnter, gm_801AA28C_OnFrame, gm_801AA688, gm_801AA6FC, gm_801AA774, gm_801AD254, gm_801AD620_OnFrame, and others visible in the diff.
Inline helpers get_master_status and get_copy_status were added near the end of gm_1A4C.c, suggesting helper extraction was used where it matched generated code better than repeated indexing.

Exact float constants and thresholds were retained in source, such as Vec3 initializers {0, 182, 0}/{0, 125, 0}, scale table entries, 300.0F, 0x1A4, 160.0f, 190.0f, and movie frame thresholds like 0x13AA/0x1374/0x157C.
Data definitions were moved from opaque symbols toward typed C arrays/structs, and symbols.txt was adjusted to keep the linker/data layout consistent.
Boolean expressions sometimes preserve compiler-like shape, e.g. repeated un_803048C0(gm_801A659C(i)) checks and ternary-to-bool forms visible in fn_801A94BC.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the slice.
The PR body says: 'Lots of these functions that are not quite matching will be 100% when split into their appropriate files.'
decomp.dev bot reported GALE01 matched code 34.94% (+0.21%, +8320 bytes), with 47 new matches and 11 improvements in unmatched functions.

Notable bot-listed new 100% matches included gm_801AE050, gm_801AA28C_OnFrame, fn_801AE948, gm_801ADB04, gm_801AA110_OnEnter, gm_801A68D8, gm_801AE640, gm_801ACD8C_OnFrame, gm_801AE848, gm_801ACF8C_OnFrame, gm_801AE74C, gm_801ACE94_OnEnter, gm_801A64A8_OnFrame, gm_801ACCA0_OnEnter, fn_801A851C, fn_801A6ACC, and gm_801A659C.

Bot-listed partial improvements included gm_801AD254 to 93.83%, gm_801AD620_OnFrame to 88.59%, fn_801AD920 to 92.56%, fn_801AA854 to 94.87%, gm_801AD088 to 80.00%, fn_801A94BC to 98.92%, fn_801A6B6C to 91.84%, gm_801ADCE4_OnEnter to 89.80%, gm_801A9D0C_OnFrame to 83.02%, gm_801AD874_OnEnter to 80.00%, and gm_801AC6D8_OnEnter to 83.06%.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that split gm_1A4C functions into their appropriate files and completed the partial matches from this PR.
Search for follow-up changes to gm_801AD254, gm_801AD620_OnFrame, fn_801AD920, fn_801AA854, and fn_801A94BC.
Audit remaining HSD_SisLib_803A611C call sites for obsolete u32 casts or missing HSD_GObj* typing.
Look for later semantic renames of gm_1A4C static data such as gm_804D67F8, gm_804D67FC, gm_804D680C, gm_803DB2EC, and gm_803DBFB4.
Compare subsequent symbol table changes around gm_803DD408/lbl_803DD408 to see whether the split local strings received semantic names.
